message:
fix(ci): support Windows drive-letter paths in QuickJS module resolver

The resolver only accepted paths starting with `/`, `./`, `../`, or
`file://`.  On Windows, absolute paths like `C:/Users/...` (after
backslash→forward-slash conversion) were rejected as "Unsupported
module specifier".

Add a check for single-letter drive prefix (`X:/`) so Windows absolute
paths are recognised by resolve_module_path().  This fixes 39 test
failures on windows-latest CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
